reasoning: The informal statement asks to show $\cos 1°$ is algebraic over $\mathbb{Q}$. One degree equals $\pi/180$ radians, so $\cos 1° = \cos(\pi/180)$, which is exactly `cos (Real.pi / 180)` in the Lean file (with `open Real`, `cos` is `Real.cos`). The predicate `IsAlgebraic ℚ x` is defined in Mathlib as the existence of a nonzero polynomial `p : ℚ[X]` with `aeval x p = 0`, which is the standard meaning of "algebraic over $\mathbb{Q}$". No extra hypotheses are added and no conclusion is weakened or strengthened, so the Lean signature matches the informal statement exactly.
